query: Recent PLA aircraft activity around Taiwan ADIZ in May-June 2026, and reported pause or drop in activity. What are typical daily counts recently and highest single-day count in 2026?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings on PLA aircraft activity around Taiwan's ADIZ in May–June 2026:

**The "pause/drop" narrative:** There was a striking ~week-long complete pause (Feb 27–Mar 5, 2026) with **zero** PLA aircraft near the ADIZ, and March 2026 recorded only **171 total sorties** — the lowest monthly figure since the Aug 2022 post-Pelosi surge. This depressed level continued through H1 2026; every month Jan–May 2026 was the lowest for that respective month since the surge began.

**Typical recent daily counts (May–June 2026):** Mostly **single digits to low double digits** (frequently 5–15 sorties), e.g., 6 (May 22), 9 (May 28), 6 (Jun 13/22), 1 (Jun 27). June 2026 totaled 214 sorties / 134 median-line crossings (~7/day). Notably, naval activity *rose* to a record June high (225 PLAN vessels) even as air activity stayed low.

**Highest single-day count in 2026:** The peak figure tied to 2026 is **130 aircraft / 90 median-line crossings** during the "Justice Mission-2025" drill (Dec 29–30, 2025, reported Jan 1, 2026) — the second-highest one-day median-line total on record. Strictly within calendar 2026, peak single days were far smaller (36 aircraft on Mar 17; ~26–30 on select Jan/Jul days).

Full structured findings with all data points and sources have been submitted.

sources.snippet: From Feb 27 to Mar 5, Taiwan MND reported no PLA aircraft operating near the ADIZ; unusually long pause.

sources.snippet: 90 PLA warplanes crossed the median line between 6am Mon and 6am Tue during Justice Mission-2025.

sources.snippet: Large-scale PLA exercise around Taiwan from 29 to 30 December 2025, the eighth major drill since Aug 2022.

sources.snippet: May 24-26 2026: ~60 aircraft flown; May 25 tracked 21 aircraft incl. Y-20 tanker refueling J-16s.

sources.snippet: May 28 2026: 9 sorties, all 9 crossed median line into central/southwestern ADIZ.

key_facts: A complete ~week-long pause occurred Feb 27–Mar 5, 2026, with Taiwan MND reporting ZERO PLA aircraft operating near the ADIZ — an unusually long, rare stretch.
March 2026 had only 171 total PLA aircraft sorties — the lowest monthly total since the Aug 2022 post-Pelosi surge (when 800 were recorded).
PLA aircraft sorties in every month Jan–May 2026 were the lowest for that respective month since the post-2022 surge began (per K. Tristan Tang / MND data).
June 2026: 214 total aircraft sorties and 134 median-line crossings for the month — roughly 7 sorties/day average, below June 2024 and 2025.
Typical recent daily counts (May–June 2026) are single-digit to low-double-digit: e.g., 6 (May 22), 9 (May 28), 6 (Jun 13), 6 (Jun 22), 1 (Jun 27).

Highest single-day count linked to 2026: 130 total PLA aircraft / 90 median-line crossings during 'Justice Mission-2025' drill Dec 29–30, 2025 (reported Jan 1, 2026) — second-highest one-day median-line total on record.
Within calendar 2026, the largest single-day figures were much smaller: 36 aircraft on Mar 17, 2026; ~26 on Jan 24 and Mar 15; ~30 on Jul 3.
PLAN naval (ship) activity rose even as air activity dropped: 225 PLAN vessels detected in June 2026 — highest June total on record, with 5 days exceeding 10 vessels.
May 25, 2026 featured 21 aircraft including a Y-20 tanker refueling J-16 fighters; ~60 aircraft flown cumulatively May 24–26.
Explanations offered for the drop/pause: China's 'Two Sessions' political calendar, Spring/Lantern Festivals, Trump-Xi summit diplomacy — but no definitive cause established.

data_points.metric: PLAN vessels detected, June 2026 (monthly, record June high)
Total PLA aircraft sorties, March 2026 (lowest month since Aug 2022)
Highest single-day PLA aircraft detected (Justice Mission-2025 drill, Dec 29-30 2025 window)
Highest single-day median-line crossings (Justice Mission-2025, second-highest on record)
Peak single-day aircraft within calendar 2026 (March peak)
Days of zero PLA aircraft near ADIZ (pause window)
